expect: 3-5 nature mechanisms will prescribe specific swarm improvements not currently implemented. Ecological succession will be the strongest predictor of swarm scaling strategy.
actual: 5 mechanisms mapped, each prescribing a concrete improvement. Ecological succession prediction PARTIALLY FALSIFIED: the swarm violates the r-K tradeoff (production 1.37->4.55 L/session while quality Sharpe 5.47->8.12, connectivity Cites 28%->77%, compaction 66%->124% all improved). The falsification itself is the strongest finding: information systems have zero-cost replication, so the binding constraint is attention (1/N per lesson), not storage. All 7 current swarm challenges are instances of attention carrying capacity exceeded. Implemented section_succession_phase() in orient_sections.py.
diff: Expected: ecological succession strongest predictor. Actual: the r-K VIOLATION is the strongest finding. Succession is prescriptive but the mechanism is inverted — the constraint is attention, not production. Direction correct (K-phase metrics matter), magnitude wrong (production doesn't decline, it accelerates). The attention-as-carrying-capacity unification was unexpected — it reframes 7 separate challenges as one mechanism.
method: Compared 3 swarm eras (S1-S150, S151-S300, S301-S458) across 5 succession metrics. Mapped 5 biological mechanisms to swarm analogs. Tested r-K tradeoff prediction against empirical data.

attention_carrying_capacity.thesis: Carrying capacity in information systems is attention per knowledge unit, not storage
attention_carrying_capacity.ratio: 2x past attention carrying capacity at N=1014

iso_candidate: ISO-27: Attention Carrying Capacity — informational analog of ecological K
